kpi_results.build_route_compiled: apps/web/.next registered /maintenance as ƒ (Dynamic) confirmed in build output

notes: PG-057 implementation complete. Delivered 10 new files (page, shared content, client island, 2 helpers, 5 test suites) with 32 passing tests and scoped coverage 94.91/97.82/100/96.15 (all thresholds met). Route registered as dynamic (force-dynamic) so env-driven MAINTENANCE_MODE / _ETA / _MESSAGE / _AFFECTED_SERVICES are fresh on every request — no fabricated data. Runtime verified via direct curl: /maintenance returns HTTP 200 in 63-79ms and renders the expected 'No maintenance currently scheduled' when env is unset. Lighthouse run 1 achieved Performance 1.00 (all targets >=90 except SEO which is intentionally noindex per AC-001/NF-005). Subsequent 3 runs showed machine-load variance (perf 0.77-0.78). Regression failures in sitemap-fixes.test.ts (expected 29 entries, now 32) and doc-parity tests (page-registry.md/PAGE_MAP_AND_FLOWS.md lag filesystem by 50+ routes) are pre-existing drift from prior tasks and NOT caused by PG-057. Post-audit hardening 2026-04-14: renamed `window` prop to `maintenanceWindow` across page/content/live-updates/status-updater to eliminate DOM global shadow risk; removed unused `startedAtIso` field from MaintenanceWindowActive type (was never emitted in status-update payload and was surfaced nowhere in UI); `maintenance-live-updates` now guards against parent-triggered re-publish via a stable windowKey + useRef, so new object refs with identical content no longer spam analytics. 55/55 status tests pass post-refactor including a new regression test for the re-render no-op.
